rules_evaluated.rule: check_platform_and_boundaries §1 — training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Email names CLI as the default ('AE101 runs on the CLI by default') and explicitly allows Desktop ('The desktop app is fine too'). Consistent with §1.
No speculative capability claims. All claims are observable install/auth behaviors students can verify directly.

rules_evaluated.rule: check_platform_and_boundaries §7 — sessions are the unit of work
rules_evaluated.evidence: Email does not use the install-level verb 'open Claude Code' in a session-work context. The one occurrence ('Open Claude Code, send it a prompt') is correctly framed as an install-verification UI-access step, not a work-session instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule: check_platform_and_boundaries §6 — known correct Claude Code facts (license tiers)
rules_evaluated.evidence: Claims 'Pro tier or Team tier' as required paid plans. Both tiers are valid for Claude Code access.

rules_evaluated.rule: check_prompts — no fenced prompt blocks present
rules_evaluated.evidence: File contains no fenced prompt blocks. One inline italic example ('hello, what version are you?') is a verify instruction, not a student-copy prompt block. No prompt design rules apply.

rules_evaluated.rule: check_research_claims — no citations or statistics present
rules_evaluated.evidence: File makes no practitioner citations, statistics, or research claims. Research compendium rules do not apply.
No skill invocations in the file.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Pro tier or Team tier required
platform_claims_checked.note: Both Claude Pro and Team plans are real paid tiers that unlock Claude Code access.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Sign in at claude.ai to confirm plan
platform_claims_checked.note: claude.ai is the correct sign-in URL for the standard consumer/team path. Page returned 403 (auth-gated), consistent with a sign-in wall — not a dead URL.

claude.com/code issues a 302 redirect to claude.ai/code. User lands at the correct install page. Non-blocking: the URL in the email is functionally correct. Minor: claude.ai/code is the canonical URL; claude.com/code is an alias. Either form is acceptable in a trainer email.

platform_claims_checked.claim: AE101 runs on the CLI by default; Desktop app also fine
platform_claims_checked.note: Consistent with platform compendium §1 (CLI + Desktop both valid for AE101). No incorrect runtime claims.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Claude Code is the tool used every session
platform_claims_checked.note: Architectural rule in platform compendium §1 confirmed — Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) is the training platform for AE101.
Valid network/filesystem pre-check; no false capability claim.

urls_sampled.note: Expected behavior — sign-in wall. Not a dead URL.
Redirects correctly to install page. Functional; not a dead URL.
Auth-gated install page. Destination is correct and reachable after login.
